revision.comment: 新しいページ：御諸別王。
revision.text:
{{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫|御諸別命}} '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのおう<ref>読みは『日本古代氏族人名辞典』御諸別王項による。</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。'''大御諸別命'''、'''御諸別命'''、'''弥母里別命'''とも。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]第1皇子）の三世孫で、[[彦狭島王]]の子である。
== 事績 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父・[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、以下の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫・現古君、（中略）、因賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫・御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫・大御諸別命の後。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には上毛野君・下毛野君の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（[[吉川弘文館]]、2010年普及版）御諸別王項
== 関連項目 == * [[毛野氏]] ** [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みもろわけのおう}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:毛野氏]]

{{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫|御諸別命}} '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのおう<ref>読みは『日本古代氏族人名辞典』御諸別王項による。</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。'''大御諸別命'''、'''御諸別命'''、'''弥母里別命'''とも。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]第1皇子）の三世孫で、[[彦狭島王]]の子である。
== 事績 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父・[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、以下の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫・現古君、（中略）、因賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫・御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫・大御諸別命の後。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には上毛野君・下毛野君の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
『[[国造本紀]]』（『[[先代旧事本紀]]』第10巻）[[針間鴨国造]]条には「上毛野と同祖の御穂別命の子・市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説がある<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（[[吉川弘文館]]、2010年普及版）御諸別王項
== 関連項目 == * [[毛野氏]] ** [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みもろわけのおう}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:毛野氏]]

{{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫|御諸別命}} '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのおう{{Sfn|御諸別王（古代氏族）|1979年}}）は、[[記紀]]に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の三世孫で、[[彦狭島王]]の子である。『[[日本書紀]]』では「御諸別王」、他文献では「大御諸別命」「御諸別命」「弥母里別命」とも表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父の[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫の現古君、（中略）、因茲賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の大御諸別命の後。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には上毛野君・下毛野君の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説がある<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>。

== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]] * [[後二子古墳]]

== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父の[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。

[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の三世孫で、[[彦狭島王]]の子である。『[[日本書紀]]』では「御諸別王」、他文献では「大御諸別命」「御諸別命」「弥母里別命」とも表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父の[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫の現古君、（中略）、因茲賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の大御諸別命の後。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には上毛野君・下毛野君の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説がある<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>。

{{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫の御諸別命|佐伯直}} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より御諸別王の想像図]] '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのおう{{Sfn|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の三世孫で、[[彦狭島王]]の子であり[[毛野氏]]の祖。『[[日本書紀]]』では「御諸別王」、他文献では「大御諸別命」「御諸別命」「弥母里別命」とも表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父の[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫の現古君、（中略）、因茲賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の大御諸別命の後。
また御諸別王の子に[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]、[[市入別命]]、[[鹿我別|鹿我別命]]がおり、それぞれが[[上毛野国造]]、[[針間鴨国造]]、[[浮田国造]]の祖であるとされる。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には上毛野君・下毛野君の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説がある<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>。

== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫の現古君、（中略）、因茲賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の大御諸別命の後。
また御諸別王の子に[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]、[[市入別命]]、[[鹿我別|鹿我別命]]がおり、それぞれが[[上毛野国造]]、[[針間鴨国造]]、[[浮田国造]]の祖であるとされる。
== 考証 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[崇神天皇]]段には[[上毛野君]]・[[下毛野君]]の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命の子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説があり<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>、実際『皇胤志』には市入別命を御諸別命の子と記している<ref name="nakata"/>。[[浮田国造]]の[[鹿我別命]]は御諸別王の孫とされるが、大荒田別命と同時代に活動し、市入別命と同じ[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に国造に定められたとあることから、大荒田別命の兄弟とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 御諸別王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大御諸別命、御諸別命、弥母里別命、御穂別命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[彦狭島王]]<ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、558、559頁。）。</ref> | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[市入別命]]<ref name="nakata"/> | 特記事項 = }} {{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫の御諸別命|佐伯直}} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より御諸別王の想像図]] '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのみこ{{Sfn|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の三世孫で、[[彦狭島王]]の子であり[[毛野氏]]の祖。『[[日本書紀]]』にのみ'''王'''の[[尊号]]で表記され、他文献では'''大御諸別命'''、'''御諸別命'''、'''弥母里別命'''（みもりわけのみこと）、'''御穂別命'''（みほわけのみこと）と'''命'''の尊号で表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父の[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫の現古君、（中略）、因茲賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の大御諸別命の後。
また御諸別王の子に[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]、[[市入別命]]、[[鹿我別|鹿我別命]]がおり、それぞれが[[上毛野国造]]、[[針間鴨国造]]、[[浮田国造]]の祖であるとされる。

『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命の子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説があり<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>、実際『皇胤志』には市入別命を御諸別命の子と記している<ref name="nakata"/>。[[浮田国造]]の[[鹿我別命]]は御諸別王の孫とされるが、大荒田別命と同時代に活動し、市入別命と同じ[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に国造に定められたとあることから、大荒田別命の兄弟とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。

{{DEFAULTSORT:みもろわけのみこ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:上野国の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝みもろわけのおう]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 御諸別王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大御諸別命、御諸別命、弥母里別命、御穂別命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[彦狭島王]]<ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、558、559頁。）。</ref> | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[市入別命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[鹿我別命]]（[[#考証|後述]]） | 特記事項 = }} {{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫の御諸別命|佐伯直}} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より御諸別王の想像図]] '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのみこ{{Sfn|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の三世孫で、[[彦狭島王]]の子であり[[毛野氏]]の祖。『[[日本書紀]]』にのみ'''王'''の[[尊号]]で表記され、他文献では'''大御諸別命'''、'''御諸別命'''、'''弥母里別命'''（みもりわけのみこと）、'''御穂別命'''（みほわけのみこと）と'''命'''の尊号で表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父の[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫の現古君、（中略）、因茲賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の大御諸別命の後。
また御諸別王の子に[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]、[[市入別命]]、[[鹿我別|鹿我別命]]（[[#考証|後述]]）がおり、それぞれが[[上毛野国造]]、[[針間鴨国造]]、[[浮田国造]]の祖であるとされる。
== 考証 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[崇神天皇]]段には[[上毛野君]]・[[下毛野君]]の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命の子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説があり<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>、実際『皇胤志』には市入別命を御諸別命の子と記している<ref name="nakata"/>。[[浮田国造]]の[[鹿我別命]]は御諸別王の孫とされるが、大荒田別命と同時代に活動し、市入別命と同じ[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に国造に定められたとあることから、大荒田別命の従兄弟とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。実際に、「国造本紀」には鹿我別命（賀我別王）を崇神天皇の五世孫としており、これに従えば大荒田別命と同世代となる。

また御諸別王の子に[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]、[[市入別命]]、[[鹿我別|鹿我別命]]（[[#考証|後述]]）がおり、それぞれが[[上毛野国造]]、[[針間鴨国造]]、[[浮田国造]]の祖であるとされる。
== 考証 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[崇神天皇]]段には[[上毛野君]]・[[下毛野君]]の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命の子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説があり<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>、実際『皇胤志』には市入別命を御諸別命の子と記している<ref name="nakata"/>。[[浮田国造]]の[[鹿我別命]]は御諸別王の孫とされるが、大荒田別命と同時代に活動し、市入別命と同じ[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に国造に定められたとあることから、大荒田別命の従兄弟とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。実際に、「国造本紀」には鹿我別命（賀我別王）を崇神天皇の五世孫としており、これに従えば大荒田別命と同世代となる。

{{DEFAULTSORT:みもろわけのみこ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:上野国の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝みもろわけのおう]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 御諸別王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大御諸別命、御諸別命、弥母里別命、御穂別命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[上毛野国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[彦狭島王]]<ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、558、559頁。）。</ref> | 兄弟 = [[夏花命]]か（[[#考証|後述]]） | 妻 = | 子 = [[大荒田別命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[市入別命]]<ref name="nakata"/>、[[鹿我別命]]（[[#考証|後述]]） | 特記事項 = }} {{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫の御諸別命|佐伯直}} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より御諸別王の想像図]] '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのみこ{{Sfn|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の三世孫で、[[彦狭島王]]の子であり[[毛野氏]]の祖。『[[日本書紀]]』にのみ'''王'''の[[尊号]]で表記され、他文献では'''大御諸別命'''、'''御諸別命'''、'''弥母里別命'''（みもりわけのみこと）、'''御穂別命'''（みほわけのみこと）と'''命'''の尊号で表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父の[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫の現古君、（中略）、因茲賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の大御諸別命の後。
また御諸別王の子に[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]、[[市入別命]]、[[鹿我別|鹿我別命]]（[[#考証|後述]]）がおり、それぞれが[[上毛野国造]]、[[針間鴨国造]]、[[浮田国造]]の祖であるとされる。
== 考証 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[崇神天皇]]段には[[上毛野君]]・[[下毛野君]]の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命の子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説があり<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>、実際『皇胤志』には市入別命を御諸別命の子と記している<ref name="nakata"/>。[[浮田国造]]の[[鹿我別命]]は御諸別王の孫とされるが、大荒田別命と同時代に活動し、市入別命と同じ[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に国造に定められたとあることから、大荒田別命の従兄弟とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。実際に、「国造本紀」には鹿我別命（賀我別王）を崇神天皇の五世孫としており、これに従えば大荒田別命と同世代となる。
『日本書紀』景行天皇紀に見える物部君の祖・夏花（夏花命）は年代から御諸別王の兄弟と推定されている<ref>宝賀寿男「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、562頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=御諸別王|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]] * [[後二子古墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みもろわけのみこ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:上野国の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝みもろわけのおう]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 御諸別王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大御諸別命、御諸別命、弥母里別命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[上毛野国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[彦狭島王]]<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | 特記事項 = }} {{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫の御諸別命|佐伯直}} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より御諸別王の想像図]] '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのみこ{{Sfn|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の三世孫で[[毛野氏]]の祖。『[[日本書紀]]』にのみ'''王'''の[[尊号]]で表記され、他文献では'''大御諸別命'''、'''御諸別命'''、'''弥母里別命'''（みもりわけのみこと）と'''命'''の尊号で表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父の[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫の現古君、（中略）、因茲賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の大御諸別命の後。
== 考証 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[崇神天皇]]段には[[上毛野君]]・[[下毛野君]]の祖として[[豊城入彦命]]の記載があるが東国には至っておらず、孫の[[彦狭島王]]も都督に任じられたが赴任途上で亡くなっている。東国に赴いたのは御諸別王が最初であり、御諸別王が実質的な毛野氏族の祖といえる<ref name="概覧">[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]（古樹紀之房間 - 古代氏族研究会公認ホームページ）。</ref>。また、御諸別命の活躍する世代は崇神天皇の次の世代にあたることから、御諸別命が豊城入彦命の曽孫であるという系譜には相当な疑問が呈されている<ref name="概覧"/>。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命の子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説があり<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>、実際『皇胤志』には市入別命を御諸別命の子と記している<ref name="nakata"/>。[[浮田国造]]の[[鹿我別命]]は御諸別王の孫とされるが、大荒田別命と同時代に活動し、市入別命と同じ[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に国造に定められたとあることから、大荒田別命の従兄弟とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。実際に、「国造本紀」には鹿我別命（賀我別王）を崇神天皇の五世孫としており、これに従えば大荒田別命と同世代となる。
『日本書紀』景行天皇紀に見える物部君の祖・夏花（夏花命）は年代から御諸別王の兄弟と推定されている<ref>宝賀寿男「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、562頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=御諸別王|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]] * [[後二子古墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みもろわけのみこ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:上野国の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝みもろわけのおう]]

{{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫の御諸別命|佐伯直}} {{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 御諸別王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大御諸別命、御諸別命、弥母里別命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[上毛野国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[彦狭島王]]<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | 特記事項 = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より御諸別王の想像図]] '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのみこ{{Sfn|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。

『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命の子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説があり<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）。</ref>、実際『皇胤志』には市入別命を御諸別命の子と記している<ref name="nakata"/>。[[浮田国造]]の[[鹿我別命]]は御諸別王の孫とされるが、大荒田別命と同時代に活動し、市入別命と同じ[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に国造に定められたとあることから、大荒田別命の従兄弟とする説がある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。実際に、「国造本紀」には鹿我別命（賀我別王）を崇神天皇の五世孫としており、これに従えば大荒田別命と同世代となる。

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:Mimorowake no Ōkimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Kofun Period Burial Mounds|ja|後二子古墳}}
[[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province people]] [[Category:Kamitsuke Clan]] [[ja:御諸別王]]

In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}

The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}

The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | Image = | Image_Size = | Image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."

According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.

From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.

Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: - Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. - Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. - Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also! == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}

== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}
