{{draft categories| [[Category:Mimorowake]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province people]] [[Category:Kamitsuke Clan]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Mimorowake]] [[ja:御諸別王]] [[sn:imorowake]]

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|12=draft}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳|12=draft}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|12=draft}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=draft}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|12=draft}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} <!--Mimorowake-->

revision.text:
{{otheruses|[[崇神天皇]]四世孫|[[景行天皇]]孫の御諸別命|佐伯直}} {{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 御諸別王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大御諸別命、御諸別命、弥母里別命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[上毛野国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]・[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[彦狭島王]]<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | 特記事項 = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より御諸別王の想像図]] '''御諸別王'''（みもろわけのみこ{{Sfn|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の三世孫で[[毛野氏]]の祖。『[[日本書紀]]』にのみ'''王'''の[[尊号]]で表記され、他文献では'''大御諸別命'''、'''御諸別命'''、'''弥母里別命'''（みもりわけのみこと）と'''命'''の尊号で表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』景行天皇56年8月条によると、任地に赴く前に亡くなった父の[[彦狭島王]]に代わり、東国統治を命じられ善政をしいたという。蝦夷の騒動に対しても速やかに平定したことや、子孫は東国にある旨が記載されている。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 摂津国 韓矢田部造 - 上毛野朝臣同祖。豊城入彦命の後。三世孫弥母里別命孫の現古君、（中略）、因茲賜韓矢田部造姓。 * 皇別 和泉国 珍県主 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の御諸別命の後。 * 皇別 和泉国 葛原部 - 佐代公同祖。豊城入彦命三世孫の大御諸別命の後。
== 考証 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命の子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説がある<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= <!-- <ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、558、559頁。）。</ref> --2022年8月7日に本文の脚注が除去されたため、エラー回避のためコメントアウトしました --> }}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=御諸別王|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|御諸別王（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[上毛野氏]] * [[後二子古墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みもろわけのみこ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:上野国の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝みもろわけのおう]]

== 考証 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」[[針間鴨国造]]条では「上毛野同祖の御穂別命の子の市入別命を国造に定めた」とあるが、この「御穂別命」は「御諸別命」の誤記とする説がある<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html 針間鴨国造（播磨）]（日本辞典）</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|refs= <!-- <ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第8節 毛野氏族 1上毛野君」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、558、559頁。）。</ref> --2022年8月7日に本文の脚注が除去されたため、エラー回避のためコメントアウトしました --> }}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|qq=}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke Clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:上毛野氏
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|qq=}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]
{{translated page|ja|御諸別王|version=91115461|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]]
[[Category:Kamitsukeno clan]]
}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|qq=}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]
{{translated page|ja|御諸別王|version=91115461|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]]
[[Category:Kamitsukeno clan]]
}}

<!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--> [[Category:上毛野氏]] [[Category:People of Kozuke Province]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:御諸別王]]

{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]

<!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--> [[Category:上毛野氏]] [[Category:People of Kozuke Province]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:御諸別王]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]
{{translated page|ja|御諸別王|version=91115461|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--><!--Mimorowake--> [[Category:Kamitsukeno clan]] [[Category:People of Kozuke Province]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:御諸別王]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] into [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]

[[Category:Completion tier 6]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御諸別王]][[zh:御諸別王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q108680370|qid=Q108680370}}; {{ill|Q17216467|qid=Q17216467}}; {{ill|Q17226573|qid=Q17226573}}|father={{ill|Q17224229|qid=Q17224229}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17230461]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御諸別王]][[zh:御諸別王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q108680370|WD=Q108680370}}; {{ill|Q17216467|WD=Q17216467}}; {{ill|Q17226573|WD=Q17226573}}|father={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17230461]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.

From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御諸別王]][[zh:御諸別王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御諸別王]][[zh:御諸別王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御諸別王]][[zh:御諸別王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q108680370|WD=Q108680370}}; {{ill|Q17216467|WD=Q17216467}}; {{ill|Q17226573|WD=Q17226573}}|father={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17230461]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'s fourth-generation descendant|[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]] from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]
{{translated page|ja|御諸別王|version=91115461|comment=Imported full ja history}}

From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.

In the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御諸別王]][[zh:御諸別王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御諸別王]][[zh:御諸別王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御諸別王]][[zh:御諸別王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q108680370|WD=Q108680370}}; {{ill|Q17216467|WD=Q17216467}}; {{ill|Q17226573|WD=Q17226573}}|father={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17230461]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'s fourth-generation descendant|{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|en|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|WD=Q317736|lt=Utagawa Kuniyoshi}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushirofutago Kofun}}

The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."

{{ill|Q17216467|WD=Q17216467}}; {{ill|Q17226573|WD=Q17226573}}|father={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|2=ja|3=彦狭島王|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|WD=Q17224229}}}} {{Otheruses|{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'s fourth-generation descendant|{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|en|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|WD=Q317736|lt=Utagawa Kuniyoshi}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

{{ill|Q17216467|WD=Q17216467}}; {{ill|Q17226573|WD=Q17226573}}|father={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|2=ja|3=彦狭島王|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|WD=Q17224229}}}} {{Otheruses|{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'s fourth-generation descendant|{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|en|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|WD=Q317736|lt=Utagawa Kuniyoshi}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Mimorowake no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushirofutago Kofun}}
{{translated page|ja|御諸別王|version=91115461|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:御諸別王]]

{{ill|Q17216467|WD=Q17216467|1=Aratawake}}; {{ill|Q17226573|WD=Q17226573|1=Kagawake}}|father={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|2=ja|3=彦狭島王|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|WD=Q17224229}}}} {{Otheruses|{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}'s fourth-generation descendant|{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s grandson Mimorowake no Mikoto|Saeki no Ason}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Mimorowake no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = Ōmimorowake no Mikoto, Mimorowake no Mikoto, Mimoriwake no Mikoto | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = [[Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Father: {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}<ref>『日本書紀』景行天皇紀</ref> | Notable_Features = }} [[File:御諸別王 Mimurowake no ookimi 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Mimorowake no Ōkimi by {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|en|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|WD=Q317736|lt=Utagawa Kuniyoshi|1=Utagawa Kuniyoshi}} from "Japan's 100 Generals."]] '''Mimorowake no Ōkimi''' (みもろわけのおおきみ{{Sfn|Mimorowake no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, dates of birth and death unknown) is a member of the [[Kofun period]] imperial lineage mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}.
He is a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} refers to him with the title of "Ōkimi" (king), while other sources use the titles "Ōmimorowake no Mikoto," "Mimorowake no Mikoto," or "Mimoriwake no Mikoto."
== Records ==
According to the 56th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Mimorowake no Ōkimi was ordered to govern the eastern provinces in place of his father, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=}}, who had died before assuming office. The text records that he ruled justly and quickly subdued rebellions among the Emishi. His descendants are noted to have settled in the eastern regions.
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Mimorowake no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Settsu Province: Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Kamitsuke no Ason. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's grandson, Gengo no Kimi, who was granted the surname Korean Yatabe no Miyatsuko. ** Izumi Province: Chinagata no Kimi - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants. ** Izumi Province: Kazurabe - Same ancestry. From Mimorowake no Mikoto's descendants.
== Analysis ==
In the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section under {{ill|Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} refers to "Mihowake no Mikoto" as the father of {{ill|Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja|市入別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiiwake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was appointed governor of Harima. Some scholars suggest this is a transcription error for Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263279.html Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko (Harima)] (Nihon Jiten).</ref>
